title: Here I am, Send me!
summary: “In the year that king Uzziah died I saw also the Lord sitting upon a throne, high and lifted up, and his train filled the temple.”(Isaiah 6:1). The ministry of Isaiah was put on hold until Uzziah the king died. Could it be that some of us cannot give ourselves fully to the Lord because some Uzziah in our lives is still living? Uzziah was the same king who did well in the eyes of God initially, but soon became lifted up in his heart and offered incense in the Temple which was the priests’ duty alone. God struck him with leprosy until he died. “And Uzziah the king was a leper unto the day of his death, and dwelt in a several house, being a leper; for he was cut off from the house of the LORD: and Jotham his son was over the king's house, judging the people of the land” (2Chron 26:21). Uzziah was king although he was a leper and Isaiah was a prophet although he was a man of unclean lips. This was a time in Israel when the king and prophet were paralyzed by their sins. Could it be that while we call ourselves Christians, pastors etc. in the church, we are actually paralyzed by our sins to be effective for the Lord Jesus Christ? If so, let us not be ashamed today to say to the Lord like Isaiah, “Here am I; send me” (Isaiah 6:8).
